author: jtraglia
content: Deposit testing with higher gas limits

author: draganrakita
content: List of found bugs that were confirmed with steel team. Clients that currently pass latest fixture will need to fix them:

author: draganrakita
content: 1. Tx create on halt/revert refund is not done (multiple tests)

author: draganrakita
content: 2. block.state_gas_used does not include eip7702 state refund. It is higher than expected. (Around 8 tests)

author: draganrakita
content: 3. State refund propagated by mistake if store 0 -> x, sstore x -> 0, revert is done in one frame. (Around 5 tests)

author: draganrakita
content:
4. Tx create collision test (Not possible to happen on chain) regular gas was not consumed (1 test)
tests/ported_static/stCreateTest/test_transaction_collision_to_empty_but_nonce.py::test_transaction_collision_to_empty_but_nonce[fork_Amsterdam-blockchain_test_from_state_test--g0-v0]"

author: ben_a_adams
content: Also is a Create+ Selfdestruct initcode; but I think test being done for that <@&1498675099480363029> ?

author: jochembrouwer
content:
I assume the wrong tests are from this release: https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/releases/tag/tests-snobal-devnet-6@v1.1.1  <@429719902363058177>? So top one from: https://github.com/ethereum/execution-spec-tests/releases 
We should thus currently wait for a new release with the fixed tests and not run current tests as the tests are faulty?

Also on https://github.com/ethereum/execution-spec-tests/tags I see (pic), the release date seems wrong moving it much lower on the releases if we check by tag (see pic). It (execution-spec-tests) seems to point to https://github.com/ethereum/execution-specs/tags (so execution-specs), which I find confusing, is there a reason for this? I would either put all testing releases (fixtures) into execution-spec-tests and all python client/tools releases into execution-specs releases 🤔 

CC <@436244295544995840>

author: draganrakita
content: Yeah `snobal-devnet-6 v1.1.0`, is newest release and those bugs are there
